title: Air-fryer sausages
subtitle: Evenly browned British bangers in 15 minutes
excerpt: Pork sausages cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 15 minutes, turning once. The circulating heat browns every side of the sausage evenly: something a frying pan achieves only if you rotate the sausages constantly. No oil needed and no popping fat.
sourceNotes: British sausages are among the most air-fried ingredients in the country. The air fryer became the preferred cooking method because it eliminates the two main complaints about pan-frying sausages: uneven browning and popping fat. The sausages cook in their own fat in the circulating air, producing an evenly browned result in 15 minutes with zero attention required.

content.text: Do not prick the sausages before air frying. Pricking causes the fat and juices to escape during cooking, which makes the sausage dry and prevents the skin from browning properly. The air fryer handles the cooking without needing any moisture to escape through holes.

Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 2 minutes. Place the {{sausages-pork}} in the basket in a single layer, not touching. Cook at 200°C for 15 minutes, turning once at the 8-minute mark, until evenly browned on all sides and cooked through. The internal temperature should reach 75°C. Serve immediately.

items.cause: Temperature too high for the sausage casing, or sausages had been pricked
items.fix: Reduce to 180°C if sausages are splitting. Do not prick. Cheaper sausages with thinner casings are more likely to burst than premium butcher sausages

items.cause: Air fryer with hot spots, or sausages touching
items.fix: Give the basket a shake at the 5-minute mark as well as turning at 8 minutes. Ensure sausages are not touching each other in the basket

items.symptom: Sausages done outside but pink inside
items.fix: Always cook from defrosted unless packaging specifies cooking from frozen. Frozen sausages need 20 to 22 minutes at 200°C. Check the internal temperature reaches 75°C

content.text: Air-fryer sausages are a breakfast and dinner staple. Serve with mashed potato and onion gravy for bangers and mash, with a full English breakfast, or in bread rolls with mustard and fried onions. The 15-minute cook time is faster than the oven and produces better browning than the grill.
